title: First Contact
description:
Amaka opens a folder on the desk. 'The night of the breach, The Griot spent twelve minutes casing the perimeter before touching anything. Tunde's team pulled the raw reconnaissance artefacts from the network capture. They are in /recon/. Not all of them are complete — the attacker used staged scans. Read what is there, follow what it points to, and tell me how they got their first live data from this host.'

You have a shell. The answer is not in the first file you open.

welcome: Captured recon artefacts mounted read-only at /recon/
Type `help` for commands, `ls -la` to list including hidden files.

files./recon/README.md:
Recovered recon artefacts from attacker egress capture, 2024-01-14 02:58–03:14 UTC.

Three scan outputs are present. They were run in sequence — fast, full, then targeted.
Not all stages completed. One output was truncated mid-run.

Analysis notes are in /recon/notes/.

Host is up (0.0031s latency).

80/tcp  open  http    nginx 1.14.0
443/tcp open  https   nginx 1.14.0

8080/tcp open     http-alt      Jetty 9.4 admin console

445/tcp  filtered smb

Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 147.32s

[scan truncated — attacker interrupted at 03:06:12]
files./recon/notes/README.md:
Analysis artefacts from post-breach review.
Two extracted files from the live Elasticsearch index at the time of breach.
